title: Drift Prevention

content.question: When's the last time you did a real system audit? Be honest.
sop: Rate your current Deviation 1-10 from your original standard, for one key system (see calculation, page 4)
Note the Time since your last real audit, in months (see calculation, page 4)
If it's been more than a quarter, schedule the audit this week, not 'someday'.
Realign the system back to standard, documenting what specifically had drifted.
Set a recurring calendar reminder so the next audit happens on schedule, not by memory.
questions: When's the last time you did a real system audit? Be honest.
What would you find if you compared this month's output quality to six months ago, side by side?
Which routine have you assumed is still working, purely because no one has complained yet?
content.equation_example: Deviation equals 5 (1-10, how far off standard you are right now), Time equals 3 (months since last audit). Drift equals 15 drift-points accumulated. Higher/rising means the gap between your standard and your reality is compounding the longer it goes unchecked. Lower/falling means regular audits are keeping drift near zero.
content.closing_statement: No news isn't the same as no drift.

caption:
Principle Nº 160: Drift Prevention

Without correction → decline.

Ask yourself: When's the last time you did a real system audit? Be honest.
